name: Põlva (Estonia)
description: A horizontal bicolour of white over green in equal widths, with the Põlva County coat of arms centred on the white upper stripe.

features.symbolism: white over green, the standard pattern shared by all 15 Estonian county flags

features.role: coat of arms of Põlva County, centred on the white upper stripe

history: Põlva County (Estonian: Põlvamaa) is one of Estonia's 15 counties. All Estonian county flags follow the same pattern: a white-over-green horizontal bicolour with the county's coat of arms centred on the white upper stripe. The county flags were established by President Konstantin Päts's decree of 7 August 1939 ("Riigi Teataja" 1939 No. 68 Art. 554), suppressed during the Soviet occupation, and restored after Estonia regained independence in 1991.
